title: Flaky Fresh Venezuelan Mackerel Bowl
description: A light, protein‑rich bowl featuring sous‑vide mackerel, quinoa, mango‑cilantro salsa, and avocado, perfect for a post‑workout meal.

ingredients.name: Mango‑cilantro‑onion salsa
ingredients.notes: diced mango, 1/4 cup chopped cilantro, 1/4 cup finely chopped red onion

instructions: Preheat a sous‑vide water bath to 125°F (52°C).
Season each mackerel fillet with ground cumin, smoked paprika, and sea salt.
Place the seasoned fillets in a vacuum‑sealed bag or a zip‑lock bag, removing as much air as possible.
Submerge the bag in the preheated water bath and cook for 45 minutes to achieve flaky texture.
While the fish cooks, rinse the quinoa under cold water.
Simmer quinoa in fish stock over medium heat until liquid is absorbed (about 10–12 minutes), then fluff with a fork.
When the sous‑vide time is complete, remove the bag, pat the fillets dry, and heat a non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat.
Add 1 teaspoon of olive oil to the skillet and sear the fillets for 30–45 seconds per side until golden brown.
Slice the seared mackerel into strips and place them atop a bed of cooked quinoa.
Top with mango‑cilantro‑onion salsa, black beans, sliced avocado, and drizzle with lime juice.
Garnish with fresh cilantro if desired and serve immediately.
